```python
# Extract all unique measurement outcomes
all_outcomes = list(counts.keys())

# For each qubit position, collect all observed values across all outcomes
qubit_values = [set() for _ in range(5)]
for outcome in all_outcomes:
    for i, bit_char in enumerate(outcome):
        qubit_values[i].add(int(bit_char))

# Check for at least one deterministic qubit (always same value)
has_deterministic = any(len(values) == 1 for values in qubit_values)

# Check for at least one probabilistic qubit (both 0 and 1 observed)
has_probabilistic = any(values == {0, 1} for values in qubit_values)

assert has_deterministic, "No qubit observed as always the same value"
assert has_probabilistic, "No qubit observed as both 0 and 1"
```

```python
# Extract all measurement outcomes and analyze per-qubit behavior
all_outcomes = []
for bitstring, count in counts.items():
    for _ in range(count):
        all_outcomes.append(bitstring)

# Analyze each qubit position
deterministic_found = False
probabilistic_found = False

for qubit_pos in range(5):
    # Get all values for this qubit across all shots
    qubit_values = set()
    for outcome in all_outcomes:
        qubit_values.add(outcome[qubit_pos])
    
    # Check if this qubit is deterministic (only one value)
    if len(qubit_values) == 1:
        deterministic_found = True
    
    # Check if this qubit is probabilistic (both 0 and 1)
    if len(qubit_values) == 2 and '0' in qubit_values and '1' in qubit_values:
        probabilistic_found = True

assert deterministic_found, "No deterministic qubit found (should have at least one qubit with single value)"
assert probabilistic_found, "No probabilistic qubit found (should have at least one qubit with both 0 and 1)"
```

```python
# Extract all measured bit strings and convert to individual qubit measurements
all_bitstrings = []
for bitstring, count in counts.items():
    all_bitstrings.extend([bitstring] * count)

# Analyze each qubit position
num_qubits = len(next(iter(counts.keys())))
deterministic_qubit_found = False
probabilistic_qubit_found = False

for qubit_pos in range(num_qubits):
    # Get all values for this qubit across all shots
    qubit_values = set(bitstring[qubit_pos] for bitstring in all_bitstrings)
    
    if len(qubit_values) == 1:
        # This qubit is always the same value (deterministic)
        deterministic_qubit_found = True
    elif len(qubit_values) == 2:
        # This qubit has both 0 and 1 values (probabilistic)
        probabilistic_qubit_found = True

assert deterministic_qubit_found, "No deterministic qubit found (always same value)"
assert probabilistic_qubit_found, "No probabilistic qubit found (both 0 and 1 values)"
```

```python
# Extract all unique bit strings from counts
bit_strings = list(counts.keys())

# Analyze each qubit position (0-4) across all measured bit strings
deterministic_qubits = []
probabilistic_qubits = []

for qubit_pos in range(5):
    # Get all observed values for this qubit position
    observed_values = set()
    for bit_string in bit_strings:
        # Bit string is in format "q4 q3 q2 q1 q0", so reverse index
        qubit_value = bit_string[4 - qubit_pos]
        observed_values.add(qubit_value)
    
    # Check if qubit is deterministic (only one value) or probabilistic (both values)
    if len(observed_values) == 1:
        deterministic_qubits.append(qubit_pos)
    elif len(observed_values) == 2:
        probabilistic_qubits.append(qubit_pos)

# Verify we have at least one deterministic and one probabilistic qubit
assert len(deterministic_qubits) >= 1, f"Expected at least one deterministic qubit, found: {deterministic_qubits}"
assert len(probabilistic_qubits) >= 1, f"Expected at least one probabilistic qubit, found: {probabilistic_qubits}"
```

    elif len(qubit_values) == 2:

# Assert we have at least one probabilistic qubit (both 0 and 1 observed)

# Extract all unique measurement outcomes and analyze each qubit position
outcomes = list(counts.keys())
num_qubits = len(outcomes[0]) if outcomes else 0

# Track values observed for each qubit position across all outcomes
qubit_values = [set() for _ in range(num_qubits)]

# Collect all observed values for each qubit position
for outcome in outcomes:
    for i, bit in enumerate(outcome):
        qubit_values[i].add(bit)

# Check for deterministic qubits (always same value)
deterministic_qubits = [i for i in range(num_qubits) if len(qubit_values[i]) == 1]

# Check for probabilistic qubits (both 0 and 1 observed)
probabilistic_qubits = [i for i in range(num_qubits) if qubit_values[i] == {'0', '1'}]

# Verify we have at least one deterministic and one probabilistic qubit
assert len(deterministic_qubits) >= 1, f"Expected at least one deterministic qubit, found {len(deterministic_qubits)}"
assert len(probabilistic_qubits) >= 1, f"Expected at least one probabilistic qubit, found {len(probabilistic_qubits)}"
```

# Extract all measurement outcomes from counts
all_outcomes = list(counts.keys())

# Track which qubits are deterministic vs probabilistic
deterministic_qubits = set()
probabilistic_qubits = set()

# Check each qubit position (0-4)
for qubit_pos in range(5):
    values_seen = set()
    
    # Look at this qubit's value across all measurement outcomes
    for outcome in all_outcomes:
        qubit_value = outcome[4 - qubit_pos]  # outcomes are reversed (qubit 0 is rightmost)
        values_seen.add(qubit_value)
    
    if len(values_seen) == 1:
        deterministic_qubits.add(qubit_pos)
    elif len(values_seen) == 2:
        probabilistic_qubits.add(qubit_pos)

# Verify we have at least one deterministic and one probabilistic qubit
assert len(deterministic_qubits) >= 1, f"Expected at least one deterministic qubit, found: {deterministic_qubits}"
assert len(probabilistic_qubits) >= 1, f"Expected at least one probabilistic qubit, found: {probabilistic_qubits}"
```

```python
# Extract all measurement outcomes from counts
all_outcomes = list(counts.keys())

# Track which qubits are deterministic (always 0 or always 1) and which are probabilistic
deterministic_qubits = []
probabilistic_qubits = []

# Check each qubit position (0 to 4)
for qubit_idx in range(5):
    values_seen = set()
    
    # Check what values this qubit takes across all outcomes
    for outcome in all_outcomes:
        # outcome is a string like "01101", qubit_idx=0 corresponds to rightmost bit
        bit_value = outcome[4 - qubit_idx]  # reverse indexing since qiskit uses little-endian
        values_seen.add(bit_value)
    
    if len(values_seen) == 1:
        deterministic_qubits.append(qubit_idx)
    elif len(values_seen) == 2:
        probabilistic_qubits.append(qubit_idx)

# Verify we have at least one deterministic and one probabilistic qubit
assert len(deterministic_qubits) >= 1, f"Expected at least one deterministic qubit, found: {deterministic_qubits}"
assert len(probabilistic_qubits) >= 1, f"Expected at least one probabilistic qubit, found: {probabilistic_qubits}"
```

```python
# Extract all measured bitstrings and organize by qubit position
all_bitstrings = []
for bitstring, count in counts.items():
    all_bitstrings.extend([bitstring] * count)

# Check each qubit position for deterministic vs probabilistic behavior
num_qubits = 5
deterministic_found = False
probabilistic_found = False

for qubit_idx in range(num_qubits):
    # Extract values for this qubit across all shots
    qubit_values = set()
    for bitstring in all_bitstrings:
        qubit_values.add(bitstring[num_qubits - 1 - qubit_idx])  # Reverse indexing for qubit position
    
    # Check if deterministic (single value) or probabilistic (both 0 and 1)
    if len(qubit_values) == 1:
        deterministic_found = True
    elif len(qubit_values) == 2 and '0' in qubit_values and '1' in qubit_values:
        probabilistic_found = True

assert deterministic_found, "No qubit found that always measures the same value"
assert probabilistic_found, "No qubit found that measures both 0 and 1"
```

```python
# Extract all observed bitstrings and analyze each qubit position
all_bitstrings = list(counts.keys())
num_qubits = len(all_bitstrings[0]) if all_bitstrings else 0

# For each qubit position, collect all observed values
qubit_values = []
for qubit_idx in range(num_qubits):
    values_for_this_qubit = set()
    for bitstring in all_bitstrings:
        values_for_this_qubit.add(bitstring[qubit_idx])
    qubit_values.append(values_for_this_qubit)

# Check if at least one qubit is deterministic (only one value observed)
has_deterministic_qubit = any(len(values) == 1 for values in qubit_values)

# Check if at least one qubit is probabilistic (both '0' and '1' observed)
has_probabilistic_qubit = any(values == {'0', '1'} for values in qubit_values)

assert has_deterministic_qubit, "No deterministic qubit found - all qubits show multiple values"
assert has_probabilistic_qubit, "No probabilistic qubit found - no qubit shows both 0 and 1"
```
